answer:
<think> To determine if both Acanthaceae and Cardamine are flowering plants, I need to identify the families they belong to. Acanthaceae is a family of flowering plants, commonly known as the acanthus family. Cardamine, on the other hand, is a genus of flowering plants in the Brassicaceae family, also known as the mustard or cabbage family. Since both families are classified under the broader category of flowering plants, I can conclude that both Acanthaceae and Cardamine are indeed flowering plants. </think>
<answer> Yes </answer>
